PHASE-34-RELEASE-CHECKLIST.NAME: MTProto Integration Stabilization Gates
PHASE-34-RELEASE-CHECKLIST.purpose: Record Phase-34 local stabilization evidence, live-only edge assumptions, rollback notes, and operator acceptance requirements for the verified-registration plus free MTProto release.

scope.included: Verified email registration, delayed KrotPN onboarding, owner-only MTProto assignment issuance, compact user/admin MTProto surfaces, backup/restore coverage, redaction review, and release handoff.
scope.excluded: Deploy/install script edits, live DNS changes, wildcard certificate issuance, shared 443 production mutation, and live Telegram client smoke without operator readiness.

gate-1.summary: Pending registration stays unauthenticated and creates no trial, VPN device, referral, or MTProto assignment before email proof.
gate-1.evidence: backend/tests/test_phase34_mtproto_integration.py covers pending, verified, replay, referral, device, subscription, and MTProtoAssignment counts.

gate-2.summary: After verified email activation, the authenticated owner endpoint creates or reuses exactly one free personal MTProto assignment.
gate-2.evidence: Owner payload includes only owner-visible server, port, secret, and tg link; base secret and salt are not exposed.

gate-3.summary: Runtime bridge and admin operations remain redacted and admin revoke stays scoped to MTProto assignment/runtime policy only.
gate-3.evidence: Phase-33 backend/admin tests and Phase-34 static smoke keep admin surfaces free of owner-only credentials.

gate-4.summary: Emergency backup/restore documentation now covers MTProto assignment rows and rotation metadata.
gate-4.evidence: docs/recovery/BACKUP-PHASE-22.xml includes mtproto_assignments counts and row invariants without exposing secret material.

gate-5.summary: krotpn.xyz DNS, trusted wildcard TLS, shared 443 route, issued SNI routing, and public Telegram client smoke passed through Phase-41 clean-deploy validation.
gate-5.evidence: Production proof now exists through Phase-41; future edge changes still require DNS/TLS readiness and rollback window.

risk-1.summary: Phase-28 legacy route/API findings are closed locally as of 2026-05-19 after V-M-004 billing route/API and V-M-022 VPN compatibility tests were aligned to canonical /api/v1 routes.
risk-1.release-decision: Keep /api/v1 billing and VPN compatibility route tests in the Phase-28/V-M-041 regression gate for future API-prefix changes.

risk-2.summary: Production Telegram acceptance requires real DNS, trusted wildcard TLS, and public edge routing.
risk-2.release-decision: Phase-41 operator-live smoke passed; keep the same proof standard for future releases.

risk-3.summary: Emergency relocation remains blocked-live until a trusted backup bundle and isolated restore target prove pg_dump/pg_restore and runtime state recovery.
risk-3.release-decision: Use Phase-22 runbooks as procedure only, not as incident-ready evidence.

database-scope.table-1: Preserve user_id, sni, credential_mode, status, rotation_marker, issued_at, created_at, updated_at, and superseded_at.
database-scope.table-2: Preserve pending lifecycle state only when the operator intentionally accepts pending-registration carryover during relocation.

restore-invariants.invariant-1: Every active MTProto assignment references an existing verified active user.
restore-invariants.invariant-2: Every active assignment SNI stays under the configured public base domain and remains unique.
restore-invariants.invariant-3: Rotation marker is preserved so restored owners either reuse the same derived credentials or enter explicit reissue-required state.
restore-invariants.invariant-4: No backup, manifest, evidence, or restore log stores derived fake-TLS secrets or full tg links.

operator-live.preflight-1: DNS for krotpn.xyz and issued wildcard SNI names points to the intended edge host.
operator-live.preflight-2: Trusted wildcard certificate validates krotpn.xyz and *.krotpn.xyz without trust bypass.
operator-live.preflight-3: Rollback window and operator access to reverse proxy/runtime controls are available before shared 443 smoke.
operator-live.smoke-2: https://krotpn.xyz, /api health, and /admin are reachable through HTTPS.
operator-live.smoke-3: One issued MTProto SNI routes to the runtime edge, and unknown SNI fails safe or falls back only as designed.
operator-live.smoke-4: One real Telegram client accepts the owner proxy link without exposing the link in shared evidence.

rollback.step-1: Before public announcement, keep the previous stable release tag and database backup bundle available.
rollback.step-2: If verified-registration activation regresses, pause new registrations and revert to the prior release while preserving pending records for operator review.
rollback.step-3: If MTProto owner issuance regresses, disable or hide the user dashboard MTProto surface and keep KrotPN VPN trial/subscription flows online.
rollback.step-4: If shared 443 route breaks HTTPS or MTProto, follow docs/edge/PHASE-32-CUTOVER.xml rollback and restore HTTPS-first behavior before reattempting MTProto edge.
rollback.step-5: Do not rotate MTProto base material during rollback unless an incident explicitly requires reissue.

redaction.rule-1: Do not paste email verification tokens, JWTs, refresh tokens, cookies, SMTP/API credentials, derived MTProto secrets, full tg links, certificate private keys, or runtime env values into evidence.
redaction.rule-2: Allowed evidence includes test names, pass/fail counts, status codes, safe hostnames, assignment counts, table-count keys, and redacted SNI suffixes.

operator-acceptance.item-1: All Phase-34 local commands pass from docs/plans/Phase-34.xml.
operator-acceptance.item-2: V-M-046 operator-live smoke is closed through Phase-41 clean-deploy validation.
operator-acceptance.item-3: Phase-28 V-M-004 and V-M-022 route/API findings are fixed locally and covered by the V-M-041 regression gate.
operator-acceptance.item-4: Release tag, commit hash, and rollback target are recorded before pushing a stable release.
